desc.overview: Controller status, online devices, UniFi sites, active alerts and network event log.
desc.traffic: WAN bandwidth history — inbound/outbound throughput per interface over 1h / 24h / 7d.
desc.threats: IDS/IPS — blocked network flows, risk levels (Low/Suspicious/Concerning) and geographic attack origins.
desc.analyse: Access point details — broadcasted SSIDs, channels, radio power and connected clients per band (2.4 / 5 / 6 GHz).
desc.nat: UniFi gateway — WAN/LAN ports and NAT port forwarding rules.
desc.clients: Connected network devices — filter by type (Wi-Fi / Ethernet), status (active/idle) and search by IP, name or MAC.
desc.switches: UniFi switch ports — link speed, POE status, RX/TX errors and port name.
desc.debug: Raw UniFi API data — used for technical diagnostics and development.

threats.title: Network Threat Analysis
threats.unavailableDesc: The UniFi controller returned no threat data.
threats.requiresIps: This feature requires Intrusion Detection (IDS/IPS) to be enabled in UniFi.
threats.noThreatsInRange: No threats detected — IPS database is empty.
threats.noThreatsHint: IPS is enabled but no events have been generated.
threats.ipsEmptyTitle: UniFi IPS has not logged any events
threats.ipsEmptyHint1: Make sure IPS is set to Block mode (not just Detect): Network → Security → Threat Management
threats.ipsEmptyHint2: Increase sensitivity (Medium or High) to catch more suspicious traffic
threats.ipsEmptyHint3: If recently enabled, wait a few minutes for the first traffic to be analyzed

bandwidth.noDataHint: Data accumulates with each poll (~30s). Check back in a few minutes.

unifi.pluginNotInstalled: UniFi plugin is not installed or configured.
unifi.pluginNotConnectedDescription: UniFi plugin is not enabled or connected.
unifi.configureFromPlugins: Enable and configure the plugin from the "Plugins" page.
unifi.natRulesManagedByGateway: NAT rules are managed by the UniFi gateway
